verifactu.title: VeriFactu
verifactu.subtitle: Invoice registry compliant with AEAT regulations 2025
verifactu.noRecordsHint: Generate the first record from a sent or paid invoice detail

cert.expiredDesc: You must upload a new certificate to continue generating VeriFactu
cert.configuredDesc: Your invoices are automatically signed with your digital certificate
cert.expiredWarning: This certificate has expired. Renew it at the FNMT and upload it again so VeriFactu keeps working.
cert.expiringWarning: Your certificate expires in {days} days. Renew it soon to avoid interrupting automatic signing.
cert.configureDesc: A digital certificate is like an electronic ID for your company. It is issued by the FNMT (Spanish Royal Mint) and allows you to sign your invoices for AEAT.
cert.haveFileDesc: I already downloaded it from the FNMT or my accountant gave it to me
cert.installedInBrowser: I have it installed on my computer or browser
cert.installedInBrowserDesc: I use it for other procedures but I don't have the .p12 file
cert.noCert: I don't have a digital certificate
cert.noCertDesc: I need to get one. I'll explain how in 4 simple steps
cert.uploadDesc: The file has extension .p12 or .pfx. You should have downloaded it from the FNMT website or from your accountant.
cert.certPasswordDesc: This is the password you set when downloading or exporting the certificate. If you don't remember it, you'll need to request a new one from the FNMT.
cert.securityNote: Your certificate is stored encrypted with AES-256. It is never shared with third parties.
cert.exportDesc: Follow your browser or system instructions to export the certificate to a .p12 or .pfx file, then upload it here.
cert.chromeStep1: Open Chrome → three dots top right → Settings
cert.chromeStep2: Find "Privacy and security" → Security → Manage certificates
cert.chromeStep3: In the "Personal" tab, select your certificate
cert.chromeStep5: Choose to save with private key and .p12 format (PKCS#12)
cert.chromeStep6: Set a password to protect it — you'll need it here
cert.windowsStep1: Press Windows+R, type certmgr.msc and press Enter
cert.windowsStep3: Right-click your certificate → All Tasks → Export
cert.windowsStep4: Wizard: Yes, export the private key → .PFX format
cert.windowsStep5: Set a password and save the file
cert.gotFileUploadNow: I already have the .p12 file — upload now
cert.obtainTitle: How to get your digital certificate
cert.obtainDesc: It's easier than it seems. In total it will take about 30-60 minutes (including the trip to the office).
cert.fnmtStep1Desc: Go to sede.fnmt.es → Certificates → Legal entity representative certificate (for companies) or Individual certificate (for freelancers with DNI). Click "Request certificate" and note down the code they give you.
cert.fnmtStep2Desc: With that code, go in person to any AEAT, Civil Registry or Social Security office. Bring your DNI or NIE and the company tax ID. The process takes 10-15 minutes.
cert.fnmtStep3Desc: After the visit, go back to sede.fnmt.es → "Download certificate". Enter the code they gave you and download it. This installs the certificate in your browser.
cert.fnmtStep4Title: Export it to .p12 and upload it here
cert.fnmtStep4Desc: With the certificate installed in your browser, follow the export guide (click on "I have it in my browser") to get the .p12 file. Then upload that file here.
cert.accountantQuestion: Does your accountant or firm handle the bookkeeping?
cert.accountantDesc: You can ask them to export the .p12 file of the certificate they already have for your company. It's the quickest option if they already have one.
cert.backHaveCert: Back — I already have the certificate

verifactuView.subtitle: Invoice registry compliant with AEAT regulations 2025
verifactuView.certNotConfiguredDesc: To sign and send invoices to AEAT you need to upload your digital certificate. It takes less than 5 minutes.
verifactuView.certExpiredDesc: Your certificate expired on {date}. Renew it to continue sending to AEAT.
verifactuView.certExpiringDesc: Renew it before {date} to avoid service interruption.
verifactuView.certActiveDesc: Valid until {date} - Invoices are signed automatically
verifactuView.regulationDesc: Since July 2025 generating the VeriFactu record is mandatory for every issued invoice. The QR code included in the invoice allows your clients to verify it at AEAT.

importData.title: Import data
importData.subtitle: Migrate your data from other software. Accepts Excel, CSV and JSON with any column names.

entitiesDesc.clients: Import your client portfolio from another CRM or ERP.
entitiesDesc.products: Import your product or service catalog.
entitiesDesc.suppliers: Import your supplier and subcontractor directory.
entitiesDesc.invoices: Import invoices from previous fiscal years to have the complete history.

importData.requiredHint: Fields marked with * are required. If you already have your own export (any columns), upload it directly — the system will ask how to map it.
importData.dropHint: Drag your file here or click to select
importData.tip1: You can upload any format — the mapping wizard guides you to assign columns
importData.tip2: Records that already exist (same name) are automatically skipped
importData.tip3: Dates accept ISO format (2024-01-15) or European format (15/01/2024)
importData.tip4: Prices use dot as decimal separator (e.g. 1210.00)
importData.tip5: Maximum 5 MB — split into batches if you have many records

columnMapper.mapHint: We suggested the mapping automatically. Correct it if any field is wrong.
